---

# 🔍 MSBuild Project Review — ContosoApp

**Scope**: 5 SDK-style `.csproj` files, 1 `.sln`, no `Directory.Build.*` or `Directory.Packages.props` files.

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely to cause real problems)

### E1. Package version drift — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

| Project | Version |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |

Services references **8.0.1** while Api and Core reference **8.0.0**. Since Api → Core and Api → Services (which → Core), NuGet will silently unify to 8.0.1 at runtime, but the compile-time reference in Api and Core is against 8.0.0. This version skew is a maintenance trap — if a breaking change slips into a patch, you'll get hard-to-diagnose runtime failures. This is exactly the kind of drift that Central Package Management (CPM) prevents.

### E2. Test package version drift between test projects

| Package | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

The two test projects are running on **different test infrastructure versions**. This can lead to:
- Different test discovery/execution behavior between projects
- Subtle bugs that reproduce in one test project but not the other
- False confidence — Api.Tests is lagging a full minor version behind on xunit

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-patterns that should be fixed)

### W1. Massive property duplication across all 5 projects

These **8 properties** are copy-pasted identically in every single `.csproj`:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact**: 40 duplicated lines across the repo. Every TFM upgrade, policy change, or metadata update requires touching 5 files — a guaranteed source of drift (as already proven by E1 and E2 above).

**Fix**: Move to a root-level `Directory.Build.props` (see Recommendation R1 below).

### W2. `DocumentationFile` duplicated + uses legacy pattern

All 3 source projects use:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

**Two issues:**
1. **Duplicated** across 3 projects — should be centralized.
2. **Legacy pattern** — the modern equivalent is simply:
   ```xml
   <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
   ```
   The SDK automatically computes `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml`. Setting `DocumentationFile` explicitly just restates the SDK default output path.

### W3. `PrintBuildInfo` target duplicated in 3 source projects

This identical target appears in Api, Core, and Services:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

Custom targets should **never** be copy-pasted across projects. Move this to `Directory.Build.targets`.

### W4. `LangVersion` set to `latest` — restates SDK default (or causes unintended behavior)

For `net8.0`, the SDK defaults `LangVersion` to `12` (C# 12). Setting `latest` maps to the latest stable version shipped with your SDK. In most cases this is identical to the default — but if you install a newer SDK (e.g., .NET 9 SDK building net8.0), `latest` would give you C# 13 features that won't be available to developers on the .NET 8 SDK. This creates an inconsistency trap.

**Recommendation**: Either remove it entirely (trust the SDK default) or pin it to an explicit version like `12` if you need consistency.

### W5. No `global.json` — SDK version is unpinned

There is no `global.json` in the repository. This means every developer and CI machine will use whatever .NET SDK happens to be installed. Combined with W4 (`LangVersion=latest`), this is a portability risk — builds may behave differently across environments.

### W6. Explicit `System.Text.Json` PackageReference on `net8.0`

Both `ContosoApp.Api` and `ContosoApp.Core` reference:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
```

`System.Text.Json` is **already included in the `net8.0` shared framework**. An explicit PackageReference overrides the framework-provided assembly with the NuGet version. This may be intentional to pick up a security patch (8.0.5 vs the original 8.0.0 in the framework), but if so, it should have a comment explaining why. Otherwise, it's unnecessary and can cause subtle assembly loading issues.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements for maintainability)

### S1. Adopt Central Package Management (CPM)

With 10 distinct packages across 5 projects and version drift already present, CPM is strongly recommended. Create a `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test infrastructure -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` drops the `Version` attribute:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit" />
```

**Result**: One place to update versions, zero chance of drift.

### S2. Create `Directory.Build.props` for shared properties

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- XML docs for src projects only -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are never packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### S3. Create `Directory.Build.targets` for shared targets

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

### S4. Mixed JSON libraries — consider standardizing

| Project | JSON library |
|---|---|
| Api | `System.Text.Json` |
| Core | `System.Text.Json` |
| Services | `Newtonsoft.Json` |

Using two different JSON serializers in the same app creates interoperability friction (different attribute sets, different behavior for edge cases). Consider standardizing on `System.Text.Json` unless there's a specific Newtonsoft-only feature needed.

### S5. Add `global.json` to pin the SDK

```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.400",
    "rollForward": "latestPatch"
  }
}
```

This ensures consistent builds across all environments.

---

## 🟢 Positive (Things done well)

- ✅ **All projects are SDK-style** — no legacy project format debt
- ✅ **Nullable reference types enabled everywhere** — good for code quality
- ✅ **`TreatWarningsAsErrors` enabled everywhere** — enforces clean code
- ✅ **`EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` enabled everywhere** — consistent style
- ✅ **Test projects correctly set `IsPackable=false`**
- ✅ **ProjectReference graph is clean** — proper layering (Api → Services → Core, Tests → src)
- ✅ **`ImplicitUsings` enabled** — reduces boilerplate

---

## 📋 Summary: Recommended actions by priority

| Priority | Action | Files to change | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | Create `Directory.Build.props` (S2) | Create 1 new file, simplify 5 `.csproj` | Eliminates 40+ duplicated lines |
| **2** | Create `Directory.Packages.props` with CPM (S1) | Create 1 new file, edit 5 `.csproj` | Fixes E1 + E2, prevents future drift |
| **3** | Create `Directory.Build.targets` (S3) | Create 1 new file, edit 3 `.csproj` | Eliminates duplicated target |
| **4** | Replace `DocumentationFile` with `GenerateDocumentationFile` (W2) | Edit 3 `.csproj` | Modernization |
| **5** | Add `global.json` (S5) | Create 1 new file | Build reproducibility |
| **6** | Remove or pin `LangVersion` (W4) | Handled by `Directory.Build.props` | Consistency |
| **7** | Audit `System.Text.Json` PackageReference (W6) | Potentially remove from 2 `.csproj` | Cleanliness |
| **8** | Standardize JSON libraries (S4) | Services refactor | Long-term maintainability |

### What the `.csproj` files should look like after cleanup

**ContosoApp.Core.csproj** (from 27 lines → ~8 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**ContosoApp.Api.csproj** (from 33 lines → ~14 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Services\ContosoApp.Services.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj** (from 26 lines → ~14 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

The total line count across all project files would drop from **143 → ~58 lines** (60% reduction), with all policy and version decisions centralized in 3 infrastructure files (`Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, `Directory.Packages.props`).

Would you like me to apply these fixes to the repository?
